diagrams.description: Journalism is the professional practice of gathering, verifying, and presenting news to inform the public. This flowchart maps its core logic: the ultimate goal is to serve the public interest and strengthen democratic discourse. It integrates core components—news gathering (reporting, investigation, sourcing), verification and ethics (fact‑checking, fairness, independence), production and editing (writing, layout, visual storytelling), and distribution channels (print, broadcast, digital). These practices are guided by major theoretical frameworks (gatekeeping, agenda‑setting, framing, social responsibility theory) and unfold through a cyclical professional process (gather → verify → produce → distribute → feedback). The result is an informed citizenry, accountable power, and a functioning public sphere.
